summary: Universal git hygiene: clean floor, no history rewrites on shared branches, everything reaches the default branch via PR (comm lane excepted).

rules.rule: Force-push the default branch — destructive rewrite of the shared timeline. Forbidden without explicit, documented exception.
Rebase commits already pushed and visible to other contributors/CI.

watch.note: golden-task growth candidate (reference §3: next incident leaves a trap)

rules.rule: Bypass hooks (--no-verify) without declaring the reason; the dirty-worktree exception exists only through the governed comm-send tool.

watch.note: declared unwatched — hook-bypass telemetry is roadmap item 6

rules.rule: Emit logs/debug/scratch files at the repo root; use the designated dirs.

Every change merged to the default branch of a multi-contributor repo goes through a PR (or equivalent). Perceived lack of impact is not an excuse. Sole exception: the create-only comm lane pushed by the governed comm-send tool.
Start every session from a clean, synced working floor; finish or abort any in-progress rebase/merge before stepping away (§4 overnight anti-pattern).
